zh.Probus, Cornwall, England: 英國英格蘭可瓦爾普侯布斯
zh.St Michael Penkevil, Cornwall, England: 英國可瓦爾聖 邁克爾 吉維爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當James Nanfan於大約1370年出生在英國可瓦爾時，他的父親Henry Nanfan42歲，母親Johanna Trethaek37歲。他和Margaret Megre至少有2個女兒。他於1424年住在英國英格蘭可瓦爾普侯布斯。他去世於1460年之後在英國可瓦爾聖 邁克爾 吉維爾。

zh-hans.Probus, Cornwall, England: 英国英格兰可瓦爾普侯布斯
zh-hans.St Michael Penkevil, Cornwall, England: 英国可瓦爾聖 邁克爾 吉維爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当James Nanfan于大约1370年出生在英国可瓦爾时，他的父亲Henry Nanfan42岁，母亲Johanna Trethaek37岁。他和Margaret Megre至少有2个女儿。他于1424年住在英国英格兰可瓦爾普侯布斯。他去世于1460年之后在英国可瓦爾聖 邁克爾 吉維爾。

nameMeanings.name: Nanfan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French, Haitian, and Mauritian: nickname from French fanfan ‘baby, (little) child’. This surname is very rare in France. It was brought to the US mostly from Haiti, and also from the Dominican Republic, where it is of Haitian origin and rare.

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When James Nanfan was born about 1370, in Cornwall, England, his father, Henry Nanfan, was 42 and his mother, Johanna Trethaek, was 37. He had at least 2 daughters with Margaret Megre. He lived in Probus, Cornwall, England, United Kingdom in 1424. He died after 1460, in St Michael Penkivel, Cornwall, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque James Nanfan est né environ 1370, à Cornouailles, Angleterre, son père, Henry Nanfan, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Johanna Trethaek, avait 37 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 filles avec Margaret Megre. Il a vécu à Probus, Cornouailles, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni en 1424. Il est décédé après 1460, à St Michael Penkivel, Cornouailles, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als James Nanfan um 1370 in Cornwall, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Henry Nanfan 42 und seine Mutter Johanna Trethaek 37. Er hatte mit Margaret Megre mindestens 2 Töchter. 1424 hat er in Probus, Cornwall, England, United Kingdom gelebt. Er starb nach 1460 in St Michael Penkivel, Cornwall, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando James Nanfan è nato il circa 1370 nel Cornwall, England, il suo padre, Henry Nanfan, aveva 42 anni e il suo madre, Johanna Trethaek, aveva 37 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figlie con Margaret Megre. Vissuto a Probus, Cornwall, Inghilterra, Regno Unito nel 1424. Morì dopo il 1460, nel St Michael Penkivel, Cornwall, England.

ja.Probus, Cornwall, England: イギリスイギリスコルンワルプロブス
ja.St Michael Penkevil, Cornwall, England: イギリスコルンワルスト ミチャエル ペンキベル
ja.lifeSummary: James Nanfanが約1370年にイギリスコルンワルで生まれた時，父Henry Nanfanは42歳，母Johanna Trethaekは37歳であった。彼はMargaret Megreとの間に少なくとも2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1424年にはイギリスイギリスコルンワルプロブスに住んでいた。彼は1460年以後にイギリスコルンワルスト ミチャエル ペンキベルで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: James Nanfan(이)가 대략 1370년 영국 코른월에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry Nanfan(은)는 42세였고, 그의 어머니 Johanna Trethaek(은)는 37세였다. 그는 Margaret Megre(와)과 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 1424년도에 영국 잉글랜드 코른월 프로버스에서 살았다. 그는 1460년 이후 영국 코른월 스트 믹하얼 펜키벨에서 사망했다.

mn.Probus, Cornwall, England: Lannbrobus, Kernev, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando James Nanfan nasceu aproximadamente 1370, em Cornwall, Inglaterra, seu pai, Henry Nanfan, tinha 42 anos e sua mãe, Johanna Trethaek, tinha 37 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhas com Margaret Megre. Ele viveu em Probus, Cornwall, Inglaterra, United Kingdom em 1424. Ele faleceu após 1460, em St Michael Penkivel, Cornwall, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда James Nanfan родился около 1370 г. в местности корнвалл, Англия, его отцу по имени Henry Nanfan было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Johanna Trethaek, 37. У него было по крайней мере 2 дочери от Margaret Megre. Он жил в местности пробус, корнвалл, Англия, Великобритания в 1424 году. Он умер после 1460 г. в местности ст мачаел пенкивел, корнвалл, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando James Nanfan nació aproximadamente 1370, en Cornwall, Inglaterra, su papá, Henry Nanfan, tenía 42 años y su mamá, Johanna Trethaek, tenía 37 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijas con Margaret Megre. Vivió en Probus, Cornwall, Inglaterra, Reino Unido en 1424. Murió después de 1460, en St Michael Penkivel, Cornwall, Inglaterra.
